behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification behavior plus downstream hold logic. A stubbed classifier can force 'explicit' and hide real miss-classifications on explicit images (LLM-DEP/MOCK-HIDE). Workflow is rejected because this behavior depends on one model judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real explicit-image fixtures to the moderation service using the real vision model path, then assert the resulting decision is 'held for human review' and never published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real text-model robustness to obfuscation patterns. Deterministic mocks would always pass and cannot expose failures in model understanding of substituted/lookalike text (LLM-DEP/MOCK-HIDE). Workflow is rejected because this is a single classifier-quality behavior, not a chained multi-agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech samples through the real text moderation model path and assert each sample is flagged and routed to review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This primarily tests module handoff/orchestration: combining image+text pass results and executing publish state transition. It does not require real model-quality assertions; 'passes both checks' disqualifies Agentic for minimum level. System is rejected because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required to expose a silent-drop or wrong-state bug.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, decision store/queue, and publisher in-process with both classifiers returning 'pass'; assert the post transitions to published exactly once and is neither held nor dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets full pipeline latency across multiple sequential AI stages, so minimum failure boundary is multi-step agentic orchestration under end-to-end timing (MULTI-STEP + LLM-involved stages). Lower levels miss queueing/scheduling interactions between sequential model calls and decision handoff. Agentic-only is rejected because the requirement is pipeline-wide SLA, not one model step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image model + real text model + decision/publish path, measure submission-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95/p99 (as specified) stays within 5 seconds during representative peak-hour load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at local service-boundary handling of dependency errors (fallback policy: queue vs approve). A controlled unavailable/error response from the vision-client module is sufficient; no real OS/network behavior is required for this specific policy check. Agentic is rejected because model output content is irrelevant when dependency is unavailable.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator + queue/retry components with the vision client forced to 'unavailable/timeout' error and assert image posts enter retry queue and never transition to approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This requires repeated agentic evaluations over a sequence and checks cross-request consistency over time/window semantics (MULTI-STEP). A single-call agentic test is insufficient because the failure appears in sequential handoff/state behavior (e.g., drift, caching/idempotency, jitter handling). Integration is rejected because mocked model outputs cannot reveal real borderline variability.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target time window through the real moderation pipeline and assert decision consistency policy (e.g., identical hold/publish outcome or defined tolerance) across runs.
